characters.physical_details: Inhabits Dominic Altieri's body from Chapter 1 onward: 31 years old, 5'10, dark brown hair, slightly crooked nose (broken as a teenager), broad hands with a scar across the left knuckle, medium build. Nate's original 2025 body: 34, thinner, lighter build, glasses, sandy hair. The physical mismatch is a source of ongoing disorientation — Nate's proprioception and body image are calibrated to his original body.

key_relationships.sarah_chen: Girlfriend (inherited from Dominic). Romantic and sexual partner. The relationship is Nate's primary emotional anchor and primary moral burden.
key_relationships.mort_lazar: Father. Alive in 2005. Nate interacts with him as a stranger named Dom. Cannot reveal the relationship.
key_relationships.linda_lazar: Mother. Alive in 2005. Seen at the hardware store. No direct significant interaction.
key_relationships.victor_kassos: Boss (inherited from Dominic). Professional authority. Source of institutional pressure.
key_relationships.ray_dunlap: Coworker/friend (inherited from Dominic). Professional ally whose loyalty is conditional.
key_relationships.tommy_altieri: Brother (inherited from Dominic). Financial dependent. Source of obligation and unexpected connection.
key_relationships.renata_altieri: Mother (inherited from Dominic). Emotional authority. Source of family obligation.
key_relationships.gail_ruiz: Diner owner at strip mall. Casual acquaintance Nate develops independently.

characters.occupation_status: In 2025: tech support worker at a mid-size company, divorced, living alone. In 2005 (as Dominic): Project Coordinator at Kassos Development, a real estate development firm.
characters.literacy_languages: English. Tech-literate in 2025 contexts but functionally illiterate in 2005 office software (different programs, different workflows). Can read and write normally.

state_transitions.change: Consciousness transferred from his 2025 body into Dominic Altieri's 2005 body. Original body destroyed/gone.
Moral framework collapses upon discovering Dominic fought the demolition. Guilt becomes the dominant emotional state.
Financially ruined — both checking and savings accounts emptied for Tommy's gambling debts.
Signs demolition paperwork, completing the transformation into the role he murdered Dominic to prevent.

characters.physical_details: 5'10, dark brown hair, slightly crooked nose, broad hands with scar across left knuckle, medium build. This body is now Nate's. Dominic as a person exists only through traces: emails, possessions, family stories, Sarah's memories, work files.

key_relationships.sarah_chen: Girlfriend. They lived together. He loved her deeply — evidenced by apartment artifacts and Sarah's stories.
key_relationships.victor_kassos: Boss and mentor. Complex relationship: Victor groomed Dominic but also constrained him.
key_relationships.renata_altieri: Mother. Close but strained by Dominic's career choices.
key_relationships.tommy_altieri: Brother. Dominic regularly bailed Tommy out financially.
key_relationships.mort_lazar: Tenant on a project Dominic administered. They never met meaningfully but Dominic knew Mort's name and advocated for his store.

characters.occupation_status: Project Coordinator at Kassos Development. Handled site assessments, zoning variances, contractor negotiations, tenant communications.
characters.literacy_languages: English. Professionally competent in real estate development, zoning law, construction management.

state_transitions.change: Consciousness annihilated by Nate's transfer. Ceases to exist as a person.

characters.availability: Dead before the main action. Present only through artifacts, emails, and other characters' memories. Never appears as a living character.

characters.physical_details: Chinese-American, medium height, dark hair worn in a practical cut (ponytail for school, down at home). Specific physical details left somewhat open for drafting — the novel sees her through Nate's perception, which is both intimate and distorted by guilt.

key_relationships.nate_lazar: Boyfriend (she believes he is Dominic). Growing awareness that something fundamental has changed.
key_relationships.dominic_altieri: Former boyfriend (unknowingly deceased). The man she chose. Her memories of Dominic are the standard against which she measures the changes she perceives.
key_relationships.renata_altieri: Dominic's mother. Relationship is warm but complicated by Renata's intensity.
key_relationships.tommy_altieri: Dominic's brother. Sarah has a practiced patience with Tommy's crises.

characters.occupation_status: High school history teacher. Works summer school in July. Regular school year September onward.
characters.literacy_languages: English. Educated, well-read. References literature and history casually.

state_transitions.change: Begins noticing behavioral changes in Dominic. Attributes them to stress.
Catches factual gaps (Cape Cod memory), shifting from behavioral suspicion to evidentiary suspicion.
Confronts Dominic/Nate. Relationship enters crisis. Sleeps on the couch.
Relationship has settled into something new — not Dominic's relationship, but a real connection with the man Nate has become.

characters.availability: Present from Chapter 1 onward. Major role in Chapters 2, 5, 10, 12, 15, 18. Background presence in most other chapters through domestic scenes.

characters.physical_details: Greek-American, stocky, full head of silver-gray hair, tanned. Dresses well but not ostentatiously — good suits, no flash. Physical presence is commanding without being large.

key_relationships.nate_lazar: Employee (believes he is Dominic). Mentor-boss relationship that becomes adversarial when Nate pushes back on the demolition.
key_relationships.dominic_altieri: Former protege. Victor valued Dominic's competence and compliance.

characters.occupation_status: Owner of Kassos Development. Politically connected — relationships with city council members, contractors, investors. The central node in the institutional machinery behind the Eastbridge demolition.
characters.literacy_languages: English, some Greek. Business-literate, politically literate.

state_transitions.change: Begins watching Dominic/Nate more closely after the failed pitch.
Delivers implicit threat to Dominic/Nate's employment. Relationship shifts from paternal to adversarial.

characters.availability: Chapters 4, 9, 14, 16. Referenced in other chapters through work context.

characters.physical_details: 5'9, thinning gray-brown hair, reading glasses on a chain, working hands — rough, scarred from decades of inventory work. Wears a canvas apron in the store. Good coat for church and formal occasions. In good health at story start; shows signs of depression and physical decline after the demolition.

key_relationships.nate_lazar: Son (unknown to him). Interacts with Nate as a stranger named Dom who works in real estate.
key_relationships.linda_lazar: Wife. They run the store together. Married 30+ years.

characters.occupation_status: Owner and operator of Lazar's Hardware, Unit 7, Eastbridge Plaza. After demolition: selling remaining stock from his garage.
characters.literacy_languages: English. High school education. Deeply knowledgeable about hardware, construction materials, and home repair.

state_transitions.change: Meets Nate/Dom at Gail's diner. First conversation.
Tells Nate/Dom about the redevelopment threat in detail. Accepts Nate's offer to look into it.
Watches the demolition. Store destroyed.
Operating from garage. Showing signs of depression — quieter, slower. In the original timeline, he kills himself in March 2006.

characters.physical_details: Small, practical, wears her hair short. Stronger than she looks. Works the back of the store — deliveries, inventory, bookkeeping.

key_relationships.mort_lazar: Husband. Business partner. Increasingly worried about his mental state after the demolition.

state_transitions.change: Present at demolition. Cries while Mort does not.
Watching Mort with careful, frightened attention.

characters.availability: Background presence. Seen at the store in Chapters 3, 13, 17, 18.

characters.physical_details: Short, solid, energetic. Gray hair she keeps styled. Always wearing an apron at home. Physically demonstrative — grabs faces, squeezes arms, kisses cheeks.

key_relationships.nate_lazar: Son (believes he is Dominic). Deep, controlling, genuine love.
key_relationships.tommy_altieri: Son. Worried about him constantly but expresses it as exasperation.

characters.occupation_status: Runs a small bakery. Location unspecified but in Bridgeport.
characters.availability: Chapters 6, 18. Referenced in other chapters through phone calls and Sunday dinner obligations.

characters.physical_details: Resembles Dominic but lighter — thinner, quicker, more animated in gesture. Charming face. Shows physical evidence of the gambling world: sometimes bruised or scratched, always slightly disheveled.

key_relationships.nate_lazar: Brother (believes he is Dominic). Financial dependent. Genuine affection underneath the transactions.
key_relationships.renata_altieri: Mother. Provokes her deliberately but loves her.

characters.occupation_status: Vaguely employed — mentions various jobs. Primary occupation is gambling, which is not going well.

state_transitions.change: First appearance. Asks for fifteen hundred, gets six hundred.
Gambling debts escalate to four thousand with physical threats. Gets three thousand from Nate/Dominic.

characters.physical_details: Tall, athletic build going soft. Dresses in business casual. Looks tired — works long hours.

key_relationships.nate_lazar: Coworker and friend (believes he is Dominic). The person at work most likely to notice the impersonation and most likely to cover for it.

characters.occupation_status: Developer at Kassos Development. Same level as Dominic. Works on different projects but shares office space.
characters.literacy_languages: English. Professionally competent in real estate development.

state_transitions.change: Loyalty strained by Nate's failed pitch to Victor.
Directly observes a professional gap: 'You didn't know who that was.'

characters.availability: Chapters 4, 9, 10, 14. Background presence in office scenes.

characters.physical_details: Latina, mid-50s, sturdy, practical. Works the counter in her own diner. Moves fast, talks fast. Wears her hair pinned up.

key_relationships.nate_lazar: Regular customer. Knows him as Dom.

characters.occupation_status: Owner and operator of Gail's Diner, Eastbridge Plaza. After demolition: reopens in a smaller space two blocks away.

state_transitions.change: Watches diner sign fall during demolition. Leaves.
Reopened in smaller space nearby.

timeline.story_start: July 11, 2005 (Nate's consciousness arrives in Dominic's body). Prologue/opening section set in 2025 before the transfer.
timeline.estimated_span: Approximately 8 months: July 2005 through February 2006. The 2025 section in Chapter 1 covers several weeks of vibe-coding compressed into the opening.

seasonal_track.season: Summer (July-August 2005). Hot, humid, Bridgeport summer. Air conditioning in the apartment, open windows at Gail's.
Fall (September-October 2005). Cooling, leaves changing, the strip mall community in its final normal months.
Late fall/early winter (November-December 2005). Cold setting in, coats, the approach of the demolition date.
Winter (December 2005 - February 2006). Cold, bare trees, the empty lot, the construction trailer.

key_events.event: Nate's consciousness transfers into Dominic's body
Nate first sees Mort alive through the hardware store window
Nate discovers the Eastbridge Plaza file on Dominic's desk
First sexual encounter between Nate (as Dominic) and Sarah
Sunday dinner at the Altieri house; Tommy asks for money (first time)
Nate reads the full Eastbridge file; discovers demolition date of December 28
First conversation with Mort at Gail's diner
Nate's failed pitch to Victor about delaying the demolition
Marco's visit; Sarah's Cape Cod question; client call exposure
Nate discovers Dominic's emails fighting the demolition
Sarah confronts Nate about the changes
Nate enters Lazar's Hardware; promises Mort to investigate the redevelopment
Victor reveals the full corruption behind the project; threatens Nate's job
Tommy's gambling debts escalate; Nate empties accounts to pay
Mort operating from garage; showing signs of depression
Original timeline: Mort's suicide (referenced, not shown)
key_events.chapter: Referenced in chapters 17-18 as the approaching danger

key_locations.name: Dominic and Sarah's apartment
key_locations.details: One-bedroom in a mid-rise apartment building. Kitchen with percolator, small living room with TV and DVR, bedroom, one bathroom. Windows XP desktop computer with dial-up internet in the living area. Approximately 15-minute drive from Eastbridge Plaza.

key_locations.name: Eastbridge Plaza (strip mall)
key_locations.details: Single-story strip mall on a commercial street. Units: nail salon, dry cleaner (Mrs. Park), Gail's Diner, barbershop (Al), bodega (Cesar), and Lazar's Hardware (Unit 7, end unit). Parking lot in front. The hardware store is the largest unit. Demolished December 28, 2005.

key_locations.name: Lazar's Hardware
key_locations.details: End unit of Eastbridge Plaza. Hand-painted sign with slightly faded L. Smell of sawdust, metal, WD-40. Wall of specialty fasteners (including left-hand threaded machine screws). Counter with register. Back room where Linda works. Mort has been here 16 years.

key_locations.name: Kassos Development office
key_locations.details: One floor of a converted warehouse. Open-plan with individual desks. Victor's private office in the corner. Conference room. Dominic's desk has the Eastbridge Plaza file. Outlook email on desktop computers.

key_locations.name: Altieri family house
key_locations.details: Small colonial in a neighborhood of similar houses. Family photos on every surface. Kitchen is the center of the house. Smells of garlic, pork, baking.

key_locations.name: Gail's Diner
key_locations.details: Inside Eastbridge Plaza. Counter with stools, a few tables. Turkey clubs, coffee. After demolition: reopens in a smaller space two blocks away.

key_locations.name: Mort's garage
key_locations.details: After the demolition, Mort sells remaining hardware stock from his home garage. Space heater, radio, plastic bins of fasteners. The post-demolition workspace.

distances: Dominic's apartment to Eastbridge Plaza: approximately 15-minute drive
Dominic's apartment to Kassos Development office: approximately 10-minute drive
Dominic's apartment to the Altieri family house: approximately 20-minute drive
Eastbridge Plaza to the Altieri house: opposite sides of the city
Mort's home/garage to the former Eastbridge Plaza site: nearby, same neighborhood

world_rules: The consciousness transfer is permanent and irreversible. The machine in 2025 was destroyed in the process. There is no way back.
Nate inhabits Dominic's physical body completely — fingerprints, face, voice, DNA. He is biologically Dominic.
Nate retains his own memories, knowledge, and personality. He has no access to Dominic's memories.

Dominic's body has its own muscle memory and physical habits that Nate does not control — reflexive responses to Sarah's touch, practiced movements at the office, physical skills Nate lacks (basketball, cooking).

14-year-old Nate Lazar exists in 2005 Bridgeport. He is a separate person, unaffected by the transfer. The novel does not explore paradox mechanics — the vibe-coded machine's physics are not understood by anyone.

2005 technology constraints: no smartphones, no social media (Facebook exists but is college-only), no GPS navigation, no streaming. Computers run Windows XP. Internet is dial-up or early broadband. Communication is via flip phone, landline, and email (Outlook). Maps are paper.
Nate cannot contact anyone in 2025. His 2025 body is gone. There is no communication channel between time periods.

power_structure.over: Dominic/Nate, Ray, all Kassos Development employees
power_structure.mechanism: Employment, mentorship, professional access. Victor controls salaries, project assignments, and career advancement. His authority is exercised through generosity and withdrawal of approval rather than direct threat.
City council rezoning approval, investor commitments, contractor relationships. The demolition is backed by a web of institutional power that no single person controls or can reverse.
Political office. His brother-in-law has the demolition contract, creating a conflict of interest that is open but unaddressed.
Gambling debts, physical intimidation. The threat is direct and personal.
Maternal authority, guilt, love. Exercised through food, attention, and emotional demands.
Knowledge of Dominic that Nate doesn't possess. Her observations and questions are a constant pressure on the impersonation.

objects.name: The vibe-coded device
objects.origin: Built in Nate's apartment using AI-assisted coding and Amazon-ordered components: copper coil, modified microwave magnetron, Raspberry Pi, custom software

objects.name: Dominic's wallet
objects.origin: Contains: driver's license, two credit cards, forty-three dollars cash
objects.status: Active throughout; cash and account balances change

objects.name: Eastbridge Plaza project file
objects.origin: Office file containing site plans, demolition timelines, tenant lists, notification letters, financial projections
objects.status: On Dominic's desk. Read by Nate in Chapters 4, 7, and referenced throughout

objects.name: Demolition notification letter
objects.origin: Formal notice to tenants. Addressed to Morton Lazar. Signed by Dominic Altieri, Project Coordinator
objects.status: In the file; read by Nate in Chapter 7

objects.name: The level
objects.status: Kept at the apartment. Symbolic — connects Nate to childhood memory of learning to use a level with his father

objects.name: Spiral notebook (Nate's dossier)
objects.origin: Purchased by Nate to track gaps in his knowledge of Dominic's life
objects.status: In active use from Chapter 10 onward

objects.name: Machine screw from demolition rubble
objects.origin: Left-hand threaded machine screw — Mort's specialty stock, found in the rubble of Eastbridge Plaza
objects.status: Picked up by Nate after demolition. Kept in his pocket

objects.name: Dominic's sent emails
objects.origin: Work correspondence from March-June 2005 showing Dominic arguing against the Eastbridge demolition
objects.status: Discovered by Nate in Chapter 11. Not a hidden archive — standard Outlook Sent Items folder accessible through normal daily use of the work computer

debts.amount: Four thousand dollars (Tommy's debt, which Nate partially covers)
debts.deadline: One week from Tommy's Chapter 15 appearance
debts.status: Three thousand paid from Nate emptying both accounts. One thousand still outstanding.

income_sources.amount: Unspecified but sufficient for the apartment, car, and modest lifestyle. Mid-level real estate developer salary in 2005 Bridgeport.
income_sources.reliability: Reliable as long as Nate keeps the job. Threatened by Victor in Chapter 14.

income_sources.amount: Twenty-two hundred dollars as of Chapter 15 (before Tommy). Zero after.
Eight hundred dollars as of Chapter 15. Zero after.

knowledge_state.knows: He killed Dominic by overwriting his consciousness. He is permanently trapped in 2005 in Dominic's body.
The strip mall demolition is scheduled for December 28, 2005 and is on Dominic's project list.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: No one — the demolition is public knowledge — but Nate's personal connection to it is hidden from everyone.

knowledge_state.knows: Dominic fought the Eastbridge demolition internally and lost. Dominic was not the villain Nate believed.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: All other characters (though Sarah and Ray would not be surprised by this information — they knew Dominic).

knowledge_state.knows: Victor's demolition project involves political corruption — Councilman Dreher's brother-in-law has the demolition contract, there are handshake agreements and committed capital.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: General public. Victor told Nate/Dominic privately.

knowledge_state.knows: Dominic has changed significantly — behavioral anomalies, factual gaps, different habits.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: She doesn't know the reason. She suspects breakdown or deception but not identity replacement.

knowledge_state.knows: Dominic is behaving strangely at work — asking basic questions, not remembering clients, making poor strategic arguments.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: He doesn't know the reason. Attributes it to personal problems.

knowledge_state.knows: A man named Dom who works in real estate has promised to look into the Eastbridge redevelopment.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Mort does not know Dom is his son from the future, or that Dom works for the company demolishing his store.

knowledge_state.knows: Dominic has been underperforming and pushing back on the Eastbridge project.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Victor does not know why — he attributes it to Dominic losing his nerve or having personal problems.

environmental_constants: Bridgeport summer heat: humid, sticky, air conditioning units humming in windows. The strip mall parking lot shimmers.
The smell of Lazar's Hardware: sawdust, metal, WD-40, with an undertone of the rubber-mat flooring.
Renata's kitchen: garlic, pork fat, baking bread, espresso. The smell is the first thing that hits when the door opens.
The flip phone's ringtone: a default polyphonic melody that Nate can't figure out how to change.
The Honda Civic: bench seats, manual windows, AM/FM radio, the Rand McNally atlas on the passenger seat.
Gail's diner: coffee, grease, the bell over the door, stools that swivel, a counter worn smooth.
The Kassos Development office: fluorescent lights, carpet tile, the hum of desktop computers, coffee maker in the break room.
Sarah's shampoo: a specific scent that Nate registers on the pillow and in the bathroom. It's the first sensory detail of his new life.
December 2005: cold, dry, bare trees. Mort's good coat. Linda's breath visible in the air at the demolition.
